5] This is chapter 64, from the Book of Aadaab.

Chapter - 6 The Etiquettes Of The Host And The Guest 115

The Etiquettes Of The Host And The Guest

6 SO His eH @ we

“Has the story reached you, of the honored guests [three angels; Jibreel (Gabriel) along with another two] of Ibraaheem (Abraham)? When they came in to him, and said, ‘Salam (peace be upon you)! He answered, ‘Salam (peace be upon you), and said: “You are a people unknown to me.” Then he turned to his household, so brought out a roasted calf [as the property of Ibraheem (Abraham) was mainly cows], and placed it before them, (saying): “Will you not eat?” (Qur‘an 51:24-27)

The Prophet # said,

Ca OW By GE

“Whosoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, then let him not harm his neighbor. Whosoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, then let him honor his guest. And whosoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, then let him speak well or remain silent,"

") Related by Bukhaaree (6018), Muslim (47), Ahmad (7571), At-Tirmidhee (1188), and Ad-Daarimee (2222).

The Manners Of Being A Host Or A Guest

1) Accepting And Answering An Invitation

In many authentic Ahadeeth, it is made clear that it is compulsory to answer and accept an invitation. For example, the Prophet said,

“The Muslim has five rights upon [another] Muslim: answering ‘As-Salaam,’ visiting the sick, following funeral [processions],

answering an invitation, and saying ‘Yarhamukallah (may Allah have mercy upon you)’ to one who sneezes.”!7!

And in another hadith, he #% said, “Answer this invitation when you are invited.

At the end of this narration, it is mentioned that, “Abdullah [Ibn ‘Umar] 4 would go to a place of invitation for a wedding and for other than a wedding, [even] while he would be fasting.”

The opinion of the majority of scholars is that it is recommended to accept all invitations, with the exception of a marriage banquet, which is compulsory for the invitee to accept. To prove that accepting an invitation for a marriage banquet is compulsory, they cite this hadith:

1] Related by Bukhaaree (1240), Muslim (2162), Ahmad (27511), AtTirmidhee (2737), An-Nassaaee (1938), Abu Daawood (5030), and Ibn Maajah (1435)

') Related by Bukhaaree (5179), Muslim (1429), At-Tirmidhee (1098), Abu Daawood (3736), Ibn Maajah (1914), Maalik (1159), and Ad-Daarimee

Chapter - 6 The Etiquettes Of The Host And The Guest 117

“The worst of food is the food of a marriage banquet: the rich are invited to it and the poor are forsaken. And whoever leaves an invitation (i.e., doesn’t accept it) has indeed disobeyed Allah and His Messenger 3.""")

d the following wording is related in some narrations from luslim and others:

oul 35 Wi ks Gol Ys yn “Those who come to it are prevented from it, and those who are invited to it refuse to come.”

Nonetheless, some scholars have listed conditions which must be ia for a Muslim to attend any gathering to which he is

invited; Shaikh Muhammad Ibn Saaleh Al-Uthaimeen listed them as follows:

3 1) When it is compulsory or Sunnah for Muslims to cut off all ties with a person, and when that person invites you, you shouldn’t accept his invitation.

The place of invitation must not contain any evil in it. If there is evil - such as music being played - and if you are able to remove the evil (for example, you have the authority to turn the tape player off), then you must attend the gathering, for two reasons: first, to answer the invitation, and second, to remove the evil. But if you are not able to remove the evil, then it becomes forbidden for you to attend.

The inviter should be Muslim; otherwise, it is not compulsory to accept his invitation. This is because the Prophet # said,

Related by Bukhaaree (5177), Muslim (1432), Ahmad (10040), Abu 2*\Daawood (3742), Ibn Maajah (1913), Maalik (1160), and Ad-Daarimee

The Book Of Manners

“The rights of a Muslim upon another Muslim are six...” And among those rights,

Question: Do invitation cards take on the same ruling as direct, verbal invitations?

Answer: If invitation cards are sent to everyone in a community, whereby the sender does not know exactly who the cards are being sent to, then it is not compulsory to accept the invitation. If,

af MKS bp “When he invites you, then answer him (ie., accept his invitation).”” The inviter must not derive his income from unlawful sources, for if you accept his invitation, that means you will eat unlawfully acquired food, which is forbidden. Other scholars, however, maintain that the sin of unlawfully derived wealth is upon the one who acquired that wealth, and not on the one who takes it from him through lawful means. Of course, this view does not. apply to things that are Haram in themselves, such as alcohol, stolen items, and so on. And this latter view is well reasoned. Answering an invitation does not involve you forsaking | another duty that is compulsory or that is more important than the invitation. If answering an invitation | involves any of that, it becomes forbidden upon you to. answer it. Answering an invitation must not involve any harm: upon you as the invitee; this occurs, for example, when answering an invitation requires you to travel and leave behind family members that are in need of your presence among them. The inviter knowingly and specifically invites you; otherwise, if the inviter issues a general invitation for everyone in a community, for instance, then you do not have to answer his invitation.

| Chapter - 6 The Etiquettes Of The Host And The Guest 119

ton other hand, you are sure or almost sure that the inviter meant you specifically by the invitation - for example, you know him fand he put your name on the card - then the ruling for a direct, lverbal invitation applies."

‘Related Issue: Just because you are fasting does not mean that you are excused from having to accept an invitation; to the ‘contrary, you must go, and though you do not necessarily have fto eat, you should invoke Allah 8% to forgive and bless your thosts. This ruling applies for both compulsory and voluntary fasts. The Messenger of Allah # said,

“If one of you is invited, then let him answer (i.e., attend), and if he is fasting, he should pray (the meaning of prayer here, as explained in the following narration, is to supplicate — for the hosts); and if he is not fasting, he should eat.’"

The following is the wording of Ahmad’s narration: ‘And if he is fasting, then he should pray - i.e., supplicate. 3)

Abu Sa’eed Al-Khudree said, “Food was prepared for the ' Prophet 3, and when it was laid down, a man said, ‘I am | fasting.’ The Messenger of Allah 2% then said,

WEhs bY SK B25 5 bi ai Gly a2f Bes

“Your brother invited you and troubled himself for you; eat and fast [another day] in its place if you wish.’")

1 Shaikh Ibn ‘Uthaimeen issued this ruling in Qaul-Al-Mufeed ‘Ala-Kitaab At-tawhid.

Pl Muslim (1431), Ahmad (7691), At-Tirmidhee (780), and Abu Daawood

" Tbn Hajr said, “Ismaa’eel Ibn Abee Uwais related it from his father from Muhammad Ibn Munkadir..., and its chain is Hasan.” (Al-Fath (4/182). And Al-Albaanee said, “Hasan. Al-Baihagee related it (4/279).” Refer to Ab-lrwaa Al-Ghaleel (7/11) hadith # 1952

2) It Is Compulsory To Honor One’s Guest

Various authentic Ahadeeth encourage a Muslim to be generous ; to his guest and establish without a doubt that it is compulsory for him, as a host, to honor him. ‘Uqbah Ibn ‘Aamir said, “We said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, you did indeed send us; then, when we reached the people, they did not receive us as their guests. What, then, do you see [that we should do]?’ The Messenger of

“Tf you arrive at the place of a people and they order for you what a guest should receive, then accept [that from them]. But if they do not do so, then take from them what a guest rightfully deserves.’"")

This wording is from At-Tirmidhee’s narration: ‘If they refuse’ that you should take other than forcibly, then take.” And in another hadith, the Prophet 2 said,

wi Ol LM Jed Jo V5 AS ty Sp

“A guest is entertained for three days, and his Jaaizah is for a day and night (In explaining this phrase, scholars have said that a "I Related by Bukhaaree (6137), Muslim (1727), Ahmad (16894), At] Tirmidhee (1589), Abu Daawood (3752), and Ibn Maajah (3676).

Chapter - 6 The Etiquettes Of The Host And The Guest 121 host should be extra generous on the first night, and then should give his guest, on the second and third night, what he normally has himself). And it is not permissible for a Muslim to stay with his brother (for more than three days) to the degree that he makes him sin.”

The Companions 4 said, ““O Messenger of Allah, and how does he make him sin?” He #% said,

“He stays with him, and he (the host) has nothing to entertain him with (so the host might end up backbiting his guest or saying bad things about him).”"")

Concerning the significance and merits of entertaining guests, An-Nawawee mentioned that scholars are in agreement; he then went on to say that scholars disagree about whether it is compulsory or Sunnah to entertain guests [when they arrive from another area]. Maalik, Ash-Shaafi’ee, and Abu Haneefah - these hold that it is Sunnah and not compulsory to entertain guests; they likened Ahadeeth that discuss entertaining guests to other similar Ahadeeth, which apparently suggest the meaning of ‘compulsory’ but really convey a meaning of ‘less than compulsory,’ such as the hadith, “Taking a shower on Friday is binding upon everyone who is past the age of puberty.” AlLaith and Ahmad, on the other hand, are of the opinion that it is compulsory to entertain a guest for at least one day and one night. And Ahmad limited that ruling t-: the people of the desert and of villages as opposed to city dweilers.
content_id:
5] Ini adalah bab ke-64, dari Kitab Adab.

Bab - 6 Adab Tuan Rumah dan Tamu 115

Adab Tuan Rumah dan Tamu

6 SO His eH @ we

"Sudahkah sampai kepadamu cerita tentang tamu-tamu yang dimuliakan [tiga malaikat; Jibril (Gabriel) beserta dua malaikat lainnya] milik Ibrahim (Abraham)? Ketika mereka masuk ke tempatnya, dan berkata, 'Salam (keselamatan atasmu)!' Ia menjawab, 'Salam (keselamatan atasmu),' dan berkata: "Kamu adalah kaum yang tidak aku kenal." Kemudian ia berpaling kepada keluarganya, lalu membawa seekor anak sapi yang dipanggang [karena harta Ibrahim (Abraham) kebanyakan adalah sapi], dan meletakkannya di hadapan mereka, (sambil berkata): "Apakah kamu tidak mau makan?" (Qur'an 51:24-27)

Nabi # bersabda,

Ca OW By GE

"Barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah dan Hari Akhir, maka janganlah ia menyakiti tetangganya. Barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah dan Hari Akhir, maka muliakanlah tamunya. Dan barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah dan Hari Akhir, maka hendaklah ia berkata baik atau diam,"

") Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (6018), Muslim (47), Ahmad (7571), At-Tirmidhee (1188), dan Ad-Daarimee (2222).

Adab Menjadi Tuan Rumah atau Tamu

1) Menerima dan Memenuhi Undangan

Dalam banyak hadith yang sahih, dijelaskan bahwa memenuhi dan menerima undangan adalah wajib. Misalnya, Nabi bersabda,

"Seorang Muslim memiliki lima hak atas [sesama] Muslim: menjawab 'As-Salaam,' menjenguk orang sakit, mengikuti [prosesi] jenazah,

memenuhi undangan, dan mengucapkan 'Yarhamukallah (semoga Allah merahmatimu)' kepada orang yang bersin."!7!

Dan dalam hadith lain, beliau # bersabda, "Penuhilah undangan ini ketika kamu diundang.

Di akhir riwayat ini disebutkan bahwa, "Abdullah [Ibn 'Umar] 4 pergi ke tempat undangan untuk walimah pernikahan maupun selainnya, [bahkan] ketika beliau sedang berpuasa."

Pendapat mayoritas ulama adalah bahwa menerima semua undangan hukumnya sunnah, kecuali walimah pernikahan, yang wajib diterima oleh yang diundang. Untuk membuktikan bahwa menerima undangan walimah pernikahan adalah wajib, mereka mengutip hadith ini:

1] Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (1240), Muslim (2162), Ahmad (27511), At-Tirmidhee (2737), An-Nassaaee (1938), Abu Daawood (5030), dan Ibn Maajah (1435)

') Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (5179), Muslim (1429), At-Tirmidhee (1098), Abu Daawood (3736), Ibn Maajah (1914), Maalik (1159), dan Ad-Daarimee

Bab - 6 Adab Tuan Rumah dan Tamu 117

"Seburuk-buruk makanan adalah makanan walimah pernikahan: orang kaya diundang ke sana sementara orang miskin diabaikan. Dan barangsiapa meninggalkan undangan (yakni, tidak menerimanya) sungguh ia telah mendurhakai Allah dan Rasul-Nya #.""")

Dan redaksi berikut diriwayatkan dalam sebagian riwayat dari Muslim dan lainnya:

oul 35 Wi ks Gol Ys yn "Mereka yang datang kepadanya dihalangi darinya, dan mereka yang diundang menolak untuk datang."

Meskipun demikian, sebagian ulama telah menyebutkan syarat-syarat yang harus dipenuhi agar seorang Muslim dapat menghadiri suatu perkumpulan yang ia diundang kepadanya; Syaikh Muhammad Ibn Saaleh Al-Uthaimeen menyebutkannya sebagai berikut:

3 1) Ketika wajib atau sunnah bagi kaum muslimin untuk memutuskan semua hubungan dengan seseorang, dan ketika orang itu mengundangmu, kamu tidak boleh menerima undangannya.

Tempat undangan tidak boleh mengandung kemungkaran di dalamnya. Jika terdapat kemungkaran — seperti musik yang dimainkan — dan jika kamu mampu menghilangkan kemungkaran tersebut (misalnya, kamu memiliki wewenang untuk mematikan pemutar kaset), maka kamu wajib menghadiri perkumpulan itu, karena dua alasan: pertama, untuk memenuhi undangan, dan kedua, untuk menghilangkan kemungkaran. Namun jika kamu tidak mampu menghilangkan kemungkaran tersebut, maka haram bagimu untuk hadir.

Pengundang haruslah seorang Muslim; jika tidak, maka tidak wajib menerima undangannya. Hal ini karena Nabi # bersabda,

Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (5177), Muslim (1432), Ahmad (10040), Abu 2*\Daawood (3742), Ibn Maajah (1913), Maalik (1160), dan Ad-Daarimee

Kitab Adab

"Hak-hak seorang Muslim atas sesama Muslim ada enam..." Dan di antara hak-hak tersebut,

Pertanyaan: Apakah kartu undangan memiliki hukum yang sama dengan undangan langsung secara lisan?

Jawaban: Jika kartu undangan dikirim kepada semua orang dalam suatu komunitas, di mana pengirim tidak mengetahui secara pasti kepada siapa kartu-kartu itu dikirimkan, maka tidak wajib untuk menerima undangan tersebut. Jika,

af MKS bp "Ketika ia mengundangmu, maka penuhilah (yakni, terima undangannya)."" Pengundang tidak boleh berpenghasilan dari sumber yang haram, karena jika kamu menerima undangannya, berarti kamu akan memakan makanan yang diperoleh secara tidak halal, yang dilarang. Namun sebagian ulama lain berpendapat bahwa dosa kekayaan yang diperoleh secara haram berada pada orang yang memperolehnya, bukan pada orang yang mengambilnya melalui cara yang halal. Tentu saja, pandangan ini tidak berlaku untuk hal-hal yang haram pada dirinya sendiri, seperti khamr, barang curian, dan sebagainya. Dan pandangan yang terakhir ini memiliki alasan yang kuat. Memenuhi undangan tidak berarti kamu meninggalkan kewajiban lain yang wajib atau yang lebih penting dari undangan tersebut. Jika memenuhi undangan melibatkan hal tersebut, maka haram bagimu untuk memenuhinya. Memenuhi undangan tidak boleh mendatangkan bahaya bagimu sebagai tamu yang diundang; hal ini terjadi, misalnya, ketika memenuhi undangan mengharuskan kamu bepergian dan meninggalkan anggota keluarga yang membutuhkan kehadiranmu di tengah mereka. Pengundang secara sadar dan spesifik mengundangmu; jika tidak, misalnya pengundang mengeluarkan undangan umum untuk semua orang dalam suatu komunitas, maka kamu tidak harus memenuhi undangannya.

| Bab - 6 Adab Tuan Rumah dan Tamu 119

Di sisi lain, kamu yakin atau hampir yakin bahwa pengundang bermaksud mengundangmu secara khusus — misalnya, kamu mengenalnya dan ia mencantumkan namamu di kartu — maka berlaku hukum undangan langsung secara lisan."

'Masalah Terkait: Hanya karena kamu sedang berpuasa tidak berarti kamu dimaafkan dari kewajiban menerima undangan; sebaliknya, kamu tetap harus pergi, dan meskipun kamu tidak harus makan, kamu hendaknya berdoa kepada Allah 8% agar mengampuni dan memberkahi tuan rumahmu. Hukum ini berlaku baik untuk puasa wajib maupun puasa sunnah. Rasulullah # bersabda,

"Jika salah seorang di antara kamu diundang, maka penuhilah (yakni, hadiri), dan jika ia sedang berpuasa, hendaklah ia shalat (makna shalat di sini, sebagaimana dijelaskan dalam riwayat berikut, adalah berdoa — untuk para tuan rumah); dan jika ia tidak sedang berpuasa, hendaklah ia makan.'"

Berikut ini adalah redaksi riwayat Ahmad: 'Dan jika ia sedang berpuasa, maka hendaklah ia shalat — yakni, berdoa. 3)

Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudree berkata, "Makanan telah disiapkan untuk Nabi #, dan ketika dihidangkan, seorang lelaki berkata, 'Aku sedang berpuasa.' Rasulullah # kemudian bersabda,

WEhs bY SK B25 5 bi ai Gly a2f Bes

"Saudaramu mengundangmu dan bersusah payah untukmu; makanlah dan puasalah [di hari lain] sebagai gantinya jika kamu mau.'")

1 Syaikh Ibn 'Uthaimeen mengeluarkan fatwa ini dalam Qaul-Al-Mufeed 'Ala-Kitaab At-tawhid.

Pl Muslim (1431), Ahmad (7691), At-Tirmidhee (780), dan Abu Daawood

" Ibn Hajr berkata, "Ismaa'eel Ibn Abee Uwais meriwayatkannya dari ayahnya dari Muhammad Ibn Munkadir..., dan sanadnya Hasan." (Al-Fath (4/182). Dan Al-Albaanee berkata, "Hasan. Al-Baihaqee meriwayatkannya (4/279)." Lihat Ab-lrwaa Al-Ghaleel (7/11) hadith # 1952

2) Wajib Memuliakan Tamu

Berbagai hadith sahih mendorong seorang Muslim untuk bersikap dermawan kepada tamunya dan menetapkan tanpa keraguan bahwa wajib baginya, sebagai tuan rumah, untuk memuliakannya. 'Uqbah Ibn 'Aamir berkata, "Kami berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, engkau memang mengutus kami; kemudian, ketika kami sampai kepada kaum tersebut, mereka tidak menyambut kami sebagai tamu mereka. Maka, apa yang engkau pandang [harus kami lakukan]?' Rasulullah

"Jika kamu tiba di tempat suatu kaum dan mereka memerintahkan untukmu apa yang seharusnya diterima tamu, maka terimalah [itu dari mereka]. Namun jika mereka tidak melakukannya, maka ambillah dari mereka apa yang menjadi hak tamu.'"

Redaksi ini berasal dari riwayat At-Tirmidhee: 'Jika mereka menolak' agar kamu mengambil selain dengan paksa, maka ambillah." Dan dalam hadith lain, Nabi # bersabda,

wi Ol LM Jed Jo V5 AS ty Sp

"Tamu dijamu selama tiga hari, dan jaa'izah-nya adalah sehari semalam (Dalam menjelaskan frasa ini, para ulama berkata bahwa tuan rumah hendaknya sangat dermawan pada malam pertama, kemudian memberikan kepada tamunya, pada malam kedua dan ketiga, apa yang biasa ia miliki sendiri). Dan tidak diperbolehkan bagi seorang Muslim untuk tinggal bersama saudaranya (lebih dari tiga hari) sampai membuatnya berdosa." 1] Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (6137), Muslim (1727), Ahmad (16894), At] Tirmidhee (1589), Abu Daawood (3752), dan Ibn Maajah (3676).

Bab - 6 Adab Tuan Rumah dan Tamu 121 Tuan rumah hendaknya sangat dermawan pada malam pertama, kemudian memberikan kepada tamunya, pada malam kedua dan ketiga, apa yang biasa ia miliki sendiri). Dan tidak diperbolehkan bagi seorang Muslim untuk tinggal bersama saudaranya (lebih dari tiga hari) sampai membuatnya berdosa."

Para Sahabat 4 berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, bagaimana ia membuatnya berdosa?" Beliau #% bersabda,

"Ia tinggal bersamanya, sementara tuan rumah tidak memiliki sesuatu untuk menjamunya (sehingga tuan rumah mungkin akhirnya menggunjing tamunya atau mengucapkan hal-hal buruk tentangnya)."

Mengenai pentingnya dan keutamaan menjamu tamu, An-Nawawee menyebutkan bahwa para ulama sepakat dalam hal ini; kemudian beliau melanjutkan bahwa para ulama berbeda pendapat tentang apakah menjamu tamu itu wajib atau Sunnah [ketika mereka datang dari daerah lain]. Maalik, Ash-Shaafi'ee, dan Abu Haneefah berpendapat bahwa menjamu tamu adalah Sunnah dan bukan wajib; mereka menyamakan Ahadeeth yang membahas tentang menjamu tamu dengan Ahadeeth serupa lainnya, yang secara lahiriah mengisyaratkan makna 'wajib' namun sebenarnya menyampaikan makna 'di bawah wajib,' seperti hadith, "Mandi pada hari Jumat adalah keharusan bagi setiap orang yang telah melewati usia baligh." Al-Laits dan Ahmad, di sisi lain, berpendapat bahwa menjamu tamu selama minimal satu hari satu malam adalah wajib. Dan Ahmad membatasi hukum tersebut pada penduduk padang pasir dan desa-desa sebagai lawan dari penduduk perkotaan.
content_ar: 5] This is الباب 64, from the الكتاب of Aadaab. الباب - 6 The Etiquettes Of The Host And The Guest 115 The Etiquettes Of The Host And The Guest 6 لذا His eH @ we “Has the story reached you, of the honored guests [three الملائكة; Jibreel (جبريل ﷺ) along with another two] of Ibraaheem (إبراهيم)? عندما they came in to him, and said, ‘Salam (peace be upon you)! رد, ‘Salam (peace be upon you), and said: “You are a people unknown to me.” Then he turned to his household, لذا brought out a roasted calf [as the property of Ibraheem (إبراهيم) was mainly cows], and placed it قبل them, (saying): “Will you not eat?” (Qur‘an 51:24-27) النبي ﷺ # said, Ca OW By GE “Whosoever believes in الله and the Last Day, then let him not harm his الجار. Whosoever believes in الله and the Last Day, then let him الشرف his guest. And whosoever believes in الله and the Last Day, then let him speak well or remain silent," ") Related by Bukhaaree (6018), مسلم (47), أحمد (7571), At-Tirmidhee (1188), and Ad-Daarimee (2222). The الآداب Of Being A Host Or A Guest 1) Accepting And Answering An Invitation In many authentic Ahadeeth, it is made clear that it is compulsory to answer and accept an invitation. على سبيل المثال, النبي ﷺ said, “The مسلم has five الحقوق upon [another] مسلم: answering ‘As-Salaam,’ visiting the sick, following funeral [processions], answering an invitation, and saying ‘Yarhamukallah (may الله have الرحمة upon you)’ to one who sneezes.”!7! And in another الحديث, he #% said, “Answer this invitation عندما you are invited. At the end of this narration, ورد that, “Abdullah [Ibn ‘عمر] 4 would go to a place of invitation for a wedding and for other than a wedding, [even] بينما he would be الصيام.” The opinion of the majority of العلماء is that يستحب to accept all invitations, with the exception of a marriage banquet, which is compulsory for the invitee to accept. To prove that accepting an invitation for a marriage banquet is compulsory, they cite this الحديث: 1] Related by Bukhaaree (1240), مسلم (2162), أحمد (27511), AtTirmidhee (2737), An-Nassaaee (1938), Abu Daawood (5030), and Ibn Maajah (1435) ') Related by Bukhaaree (5179), مسلم (1429), At-Tirmidhee (1098), Abu Daawood (3736), Ibn Maajah (1914), Maalik (1159), and Ad-Daarimee الباب - 6 The Etiquettes Of The Host And The Guest 117 “The worst of food is the food of a marriage banquet: the rich are invited to it and the poor are forsaken. And whoever leaves an invitation (i.e., doesn’t accept it) has indeed disobeyed الله and His Messenger 3.""") d the following wording is related in some narrations from luslim and others: oul 35 Wi ks Gol Ys yn “Those who come to it are prevented from it, and those who are invited to it refuse to come.” Nonetheless, some العلماء have listed conditions which must be ia for a مسلم to attend any gathering to which he is invited; Shaikh محمد ﷺ Ibn Saaleh Al-Uthaimeen listed them as follows: 3 1) عندما it is compulsory or السنة for المسلمون to cut off all ties with a person, and عندما that person invites you, you shouldn’t accept his invitation. The place of invitation must not contain any الشر in it. إذا there is الشر - مثل music being played - and إذا you are able to remove the الشر (على سبيل المثال, you have the authority to turn the tape player off), then you must attend the gathering, for two reasons: أولاً, to answer the invitation, and ثانياً, to remove the الشر. But إذا you are not able to remove the الشر, then it becomes forbidden for you to attend. The inviter should be مسلم; otherwise, it is not compulsory to accept his invitation. This is لأن النبي ﷺ # said, Related by Bukhaaree (5177), مسلم (1432), أحمد (10040), Abu 2*\Daawood (3742), Ibn Maajah (1913), Maalik (1160), and Ad-Daarimee The الكتاب Of الآداب “The الحقوق of a مسلم upon another مسلم are six...” And among those الحقوق, Question: Do invitation cards take on the same ruling as direct, verbal invitations? Answer: إذا invitation cards are sent to everyone in a الجماعة, whereby the sender does not know exactly who the cards are being sent to, then it is not compulsory to accept the invitation. إذا, af MKS bp “عندما he invites you, then answer him (ie., accept his invitation).”” The inviter must not derive his income from unlawful sources, for إذا you accept his invitation, that means you will eat unlawfully acquired food, which is forbidden. Other العلماء, غير أن, maintain that the الذنب of unlawfully derived الثروة is upon the one who acquired that الثروة, and not on the one who takes it from him through lawful means. Of course, this view does not. apply to things that are الحرام in themselves, مثل alcohol, stolen items, and لذا on. And this latter view is well reasoned. Answering an invitation does not involve you forsaking | another الواجب that is compulsory or that is more المهم than the invitation. إذا answering an invitation | involves any of that, it becomes forbidden upon you to. answer it. Answering an invitation must not involve any harm: upon you as the invitee; this occurs, على سبيل المثال, عندما answering an invitation requires you to travel and leave behind الأسرة members that are in need of your presence among them. The inviter knowingly and specifically invites you; otherwise, إذا the inviter issues a general invitation for everyone in a الجماعة, على سبيل المثال, then you do not have to answer his invitation. | الباب - 6 The Etiquettes Of The Host And The Guest 119 ton other hand, you are sure or almost sure that the inviter meant you specifically by the invitation - على سبيل المثال, you know him fand he put your name on the card - then the ruling for a direct, lverbal invitation applies." ‘Related Issue: Just لأن you are الصيام does not mean that you are excused from having to accept an invitation; to the ‘contrary, you must go, and though you do not necessarily have fto eat, you should invoke الله 8% to forgive and bless your thosts. This ruling applies for both compulsory and voluntary fasts. الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, “إذا one of you is invited, then let him answer (i.e., attend), and إذا he is الصيام, he should pray (the meaning of الصلاة here, as explained in the following narration, is to supplicate — for the hosts); and إذا he is not الصيام, he should eat.’" The following is the wording of أحمد’s narration: ‘And إذا he is الصيام, then he should pray - i.e., supplicate. 3) Abu Sa’eed Al-Khudree said, “Food was prepared for the ' النبي 3, and عندما it was laid down, a man said, ‘I am | الصيام.’ الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% then said, WEhs bY SK B25 5 bi ai Gly a2f Bes “Your الأخ invited you and troubled himself for you; eat and fast [another day] in its place إذا you wish.’") 1 Shaikh Ibn ‘Uthaimeen issued this ruling in Qaul-Al-Mufeed ‘Ala-Kitaab At-tawhid. And Al-Albaanee said, “حسن. Al-Baihagee related it (4/279).” Refer to Ab-lrwaa Al-Ghaleel (7/11) الحديث # 1952 2) It Is Compulsory To الشرف One’s Guest Various authentic Ahadeeth encourage a مسلم to be generous ; to his guest and establish without a doubt that it is compulsory for him, as a host, to الشرف him. ‘Uqbah Ibn ‘Aamir said, “We said, ‘O Messenger of الله, you did indeed send us; then, عندما we reached the people, they did not receive us as their guests. What, then, do you see [that ينبغي لنا do]?’ الرسول ﷺ of “Tf you arrive at the place of a people and they order for you what a guest should receive, then accept [that from them]. But إذا they do not do لذا, then take from them what a guest rightfully deserves.’"") This wording is from At-Tirmidhee’s narration: ‘إذا they refuse’ that you should take other than forcibly, then take.” And in another الحديث, النبي ﷺ 2 said, wi Ol LM Jed Jo V5 AS ty Sp “A guest is entertained for three days, and his Jaaizah is for a day and night (In explaining this phrase, العلماء have said that a "I Related by Bukhaaree (6137), مسلم (1727), أحمد (16894), At] Tirmidhee (1589), Abu Daawood (3752), and Ibn Maajah (3676). الباب - 6 The Etiquettes Of The Host And The Guest 121 host should be extra generous on the أولاً night, and then should give his guest, on the ثانياً and ثالثاً night, what he normally has himself). And لا يجوز for a مسلم to stay with his الأخ (for more than three days) to the degree that he makes him الذنب.” The الصحابة 4 said, ““O Messenger of الله, and how does he make him الذنب?” He #% said, “He stays with him, and he (the host) has nothing to entertain him with (لذا the host might end up backbiting his guest or saying bad things about him).”"") فيما يتعلق بـ the significance and merits of entertaining guests, An-Nawawee mentioned that العلماء are in agreement; he then went on to say that العلماء disagree about whether it is compulsory or السنة to entertain guests [عندما they arrive from another area]. Maalik, Ash-Shaafi’ee, and Abu Haneefah - these hold that it is السنة and not compulsory to entertain guests; they likened Ahadeeth that discuss entertaining guests to other similar Ahadeeth, which apparently suggest the meaning of ‘compulsory’ but really convey a meaning of ‘less than compulsory,’ مثل the الحديث, “Taking a shower on Friday is binding upon everyone who is past the age of puberty.” AlLaith and أحمد, من ناحية أخرى, are of the opinion that it is compulsory to entertain a guest for at least يوماً ما and one night. And أحمد limited that ruling t-: the people of the desert and of villages as opposed to city dweilers.
